meta.description: Curated rail-accessible hidden gem destinations across the UK

fields.highlights: Array of strings — 3-5 bullet points on what to do/see.
fields.advance_fare_from_gbp: Number — check Trainline/National Rail and update periodically.
fields.direct: Boolean — set false if a change is required.

destinations.name: Eynsford
destinations.description: Quaint Kent village with a ford, medieval bridge and riverside walks; feels like stepping back in time.

destinations.name: Arundel
destinations.description: Charming market town with a dramatic castle, cathedral and scenic river walks.

destinations.name: Amberley
destinations.description: Tiny South Downs village surrounded by hills and walking trails straight from the station.

destinations.name: Lewes
destinations.description: Underrated town with cobbled streets, a castle and access to the South Downs.

destinations.name: Rye
destinations.description: Medieval town near the sea with cobbled streets and access to Camber Sands.

destinations.name: Whitstable
destinations.description: Laid-back coastal town known for oysters, beach huts and relaxed vibes.

destinations.name: Ely
destinations.description: Small cathedral city with peaceful riverside walks and big-sky fenland views.

destinations.name: Marlow
destinations.description: Elegant Thames-side town with scenic walks and strong food scene.

destinations.name: Bury St Edmunds
destinations.description: Underrated Suffolk town with abbey ruins, gardens and a relaxed feel.

destinations.name: Deal
destinations.description: Less crowded alternative to Brighton with a long pier and calm beach atmosphere.

destinations.name: Seaford (Seven Sisters)
destinations.description: Gateway to dramatic white cliffs and one of the best coastal walks in England.

destinations.name: Broadstairs
destinations.description: Classic seaside town with sandy beaches and a slower pace than nearby hotspots.

destinations.name: Chichester
destinations.description: Compact cathedral city near the South Downs and coast.

destinations.name: Winchester
destinations.description: Beautiful small city with a cathedral, river walks and countryside nearby.

destinations.name: Guildford
destinations.description: Easy-access town with cobbled high street and nearby Surrey Hills walking.

destinations.name: St Albans
destinations.description: Roman history, cathedral and green parks just outside London.

destinations.name: Folkestone
destinations.description: Up-and-coming coastal town with cliffs, harbour arm and creative vibe.

destinations.name: Hastings
destinations.description: Edgier seaside town with cliffs, old town and wild coastal scenery.

destinations.name: Henley-on-Thames
destinations.description: Classic English riverside town with boating and relaxed countryside feel.

destinations.name: Harrogate
destinations.description: Stylish spa town with gardens, tea rooms and easy countryside access.

destinations.name: Knaresborough
destinations.description: Clifftop castle ruins and dramatic river gorge views.

destinations.name: Leeds
destinations.description: Bustling nearby city with strong food, culture and nightlife.

destinations.name: Skipton
destinations.description: Gateway to the Yorkshire Dales with a medieval castle and canal walks.

destinations.name: Malton
destinations.description: Small foodie town known as Yorkshire's culinary capital.

destinations.name: Hull
destinations.description: Underrated port city with museums and regenerated waterfront.

destinations.name: Hebden Bridge
destinations.description: Bohemian valley town packed with cafes, independent shops and hills.

destinations.name: Buxton
destinations.description: Historic spa town in the Peak District with grand architecture.

destinations.name: Liverpool
destinations.description: Iconic waterfront city with museums, music history and docks.

destinations.name: Chester
destinations.description: Roman city with preserved walls and Tudor-style architecture.

destinations.name: Edale
destinations.description: Gateway village for serious Peak District hiking routes.

destinations.name: Lancaster
destinations.description: Compact city with castle, canal and university vibe.

destinations.name: Southport
destinations.description: Classic Victorian seaside town with long beaches.

destinations.name: Manchester
destinations.description: Major northern city with strong arts and music scene.

destinations.name: Conwy
destinations.description: Medieval walled town with one of Britain's best castles.

destinations.name: Formby
destinations.description: National Trust dunes and pine forests right by the sea.

destinations.name: Llandudno
destinations.description: Classic Welsh seaside resort with pier and mountain backdrop.

destinations.name: York
destinations.description: Iconic medieval city with compact centre and huge history.

destinations.name: Ilkley
destinations.description: Gateway to Ilkley Moor with riverside walks and hills.

destinations.name: Abergavenny
destinations.description: Gateway to the Brecon Beacons and one of Wales's great foodie towns. Dramatic mountain backdrop, world-class food festival and rugged hiking straight from town.

destinations.name: Bath
destinations.description: UNESCO World Heritage city with Roman baths, jaw-dropping Georgian architecture and one of England's best independent food scenes. Just 12 minutes from Bristol.

destinations.name: Bradford-on-Avon
destinations.description: Tiny honey-stone town on the River Avon with a Saxon church, medieval tithe barn and the famous Bridge Tea Rooms. Utterly unspoilt.

destinations.name: Brockenhurst
destinations.description: The gateway to the New Forest — ponies roam the streets and wild swimming holes are a short walk from the station. Remarkably easy to reach.

destinations.name: Cardiff
destinations.description: Welsh capital with a fairy-tale castle in the city centre, excellent National Museum, vibrant Bay and beautiful Victorian arcades to explore.

destinations.name: Cheltenham
destinations.description: Elegant Regency spa town and the cultural gateway to the Cotswolds — famous for its festivals, Pittville Park and the annual Gold Cup horse races.

destinations.name: Exeter
destinations.description: Ancient city with medieval cathedral, Roman walls and underground passages. A genuine hidden gem with less than a tenth of Bath's visitor numbers.

destinations.name: Faversham
destinations.description: Tiny Kent market town with Britain's oldest brewery, medieval guildhall and beautiful creekside walks. Consistently overlooked in favour of Canterbury.

destinations.name: Frome
destinations.description: Once-forgotten Somerset market town now named one of Britain's coolest. Steep cobbled streets, independent traders, a thriving arts scene and no chains.

destinations.name: Gloucester
destinations.description: Underrated cathedral city with stunning Victorian docks, medieval lanes and a cathedral whose cloister doubled as Hogwarts. Less visited than it deserves.

destinations.name: Margate
destinations.description: Edgy Kentish seaside town with Turner Contemporary gallery, retro arcades and a cool independent food scene. Often called 'Shoreditch-on-Sea'.

destinations.name: Norwich
destinations.description: Underrated medieval city with over 30 surviving medieval churches, a magnificent Norman castle and winding independent-shop-filled lanes.

destinations.name: Oxford
destinations.description: The dreaming spires city — more than its famous colleges. Beautiful meadows, the Bodleian library, hidden gardens and Morse filming locations around every corner.

destinations.name: Rochester
destinations.description: Compact Medway city with a dramatic Norman castle, cathedral and deep Dickens connections. Far less visited than its neighbour Canterbury.

destinations.name: Saffron Walden
destinations.description: Achingly pretty Essex market town with timber-framed buildings, a hedge maze, Bridge End Garden and a slower pace than almost anywhere this close to London.

destinations.name: Salisbury
destinations.description: Medieval city with England's tallest cathedral spire and easy access to Stonehenge. Far quieter and more authentic than nearby Bath.

destinations.name: Sandwich
destinations.description: One of England's best-preserved medieval towns, barely touched since the 15th century. Toll bridges, ancient walls and the odd sense of having stepped centuries back.

destinations.name: Tenterden
destinations.description: Pretty Wealden town with wide high street, steam railway and easy access to Romney Marsh. Feels genuinely off the tourist trail.

destinations.name: Weston-super-Mare
destinations.description: Classic British seaside town with huge sandy beaches, the Grand Pier and an unexpected street art scene. Sandcastle competitions in summer, enormous skies year-round.
